pi_items.source: Task description and export_result.sh explicitly specify this exact path.
Task description and export_result.sh explicitly specify this exact path.
Task description requires 1401m. Independently confirmed via Wikipedia that the real-world Spaceport America elevation is 1401m (4595 ft).
Task description requires 5 degrees. Mathematically converted to ~0.087266 radians, which matches OpenRocket's internal XML representation for angles.
Task description requires 32.99° N. Independently confirmed via Wikipedia that Spaceport America is at 32.99° N.
Task description requires -106.97° W. Independently confirmed via Wikipedia that Spaceport America is at -106.97° W.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a real open-source example rocket file from the OpenRocket repository. The target launch site parameters exactly correspond to the real-world location of Spaceport America: 1401 meters elevation, 32.99° N latitude, and -106.97° W longitude. The requested 5-degree launch rod angle correctly converts to the 0.087266 radians expected by OpenRocket's internal XML format. All target file paths, environmental variables (8 m/s wind, 1.8m rod, 15% turbulence), and the 60-point verifier threshold have been verified against the explicit task instructions and script constants.
